requirement.text: System shall allow providers to prescribe medications electronically and select the patient's preferred pharmacy from a nationwide directory.

test_cases.description: Verify provider can electronically prescribe a medication successfully.
test_cases.setup: EHR test environment; test provider logged in; test patient chart open.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Navigate to Orders -> Medications.
Step: 2. Search and select Amoxicillin.
Step: 3. Enter dosage and frequency.
Step: 4. Click 'Sign & Send'.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Medication order is saved to the patient record.
ExpectedResult: 2. Order status updates to 'Sent' in the clinical workflow.

rationale: The test suite verifies the electronic prescription submission functionality but completely omits testing the selection of a preferred pharmacy from the nationwide directory, leaving a critical functional coverage gap.

requirement.text: The patient portal shall display abnormal lab results in red text and include a direct link to schedule a follow-up appointment.

test_cases.description: Verify abnormal lab results are styled correctly in the patient portal.
test_cases.setup: Patient portal test environment; test patient account with a newly resulted abnormal lipid panel.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Log into patient portal.
Step: 2. Navigate to 'Test Results'.
Step: 3. Open the abnormal lipid panel report.
Step: 4. Inspect the UI styling of the out-of-range values.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. The abnormal lab result values are rendered in red text.
ExpectedResult: 2. A warning icon is displayed next to the out-of-range metric.

rationale: The test suite confirms the visual indicator (red text) for abnormal results but fails to verify the functional presence and routing of the required follow-up appointment scheduling link.

requirement.text: The EHR shall automatically import heart rate, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation (SpO2) from connected continuous vitals monitors every 5 minutes.

test_cases.description: Verify automated vitals polling for heart rate and blood pressure.
test_cases.setup: EHR integration sandbox; continuous vitals monitor simulator connected to patient bed; patient admitted.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Start the vitals simulator broadcasting HR and BP.
Step: 2. Wait 5 minutes.
Step: 3. Refresh the patient's flowsheet in the EHR.
Step: 4. Verify timestamp of the new data entry.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Heart rate data populates in the flowsheet.
ExpectedResult: 2. Blood pressure data populates in the flowsheet.
ExpectedResult: 3. The data timestamp aligns with the 5-minute automated polling interval.

rationale: The test suite successfully covers heart rate and blood pressure data ingestion at the required interval, but completely misses the functional requirement to import oxygen saturation (SpO2) data.

requirement.text: The telehealth module must support secure HD video consultations with an integrated text chat feature allowing providers and patients to share links and documents.

test_cases.description: Verify end-to-end secure HD video connection between provider and patient.
test_cases.setup: Telehealth test environment; two separate devices (provider desktop, patient mobile); mock appointment scheduled.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Provider joins virtual waiting room.
Step: 2. Patient clicks join link from SMS reminder.
Step: 3. Provider admits patient.
Step: 4. Verify video and audio streams.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Provider and patient connect successfully.
ExpectedResult: 2. Video resolution negotiates to HD (720p minimum).
ExpectedResult: 3. Audio is clear with no severe latency.

rationale: While the primary video consultation functionality is verified, the integrated text chat feature for sharing links and documents is entirely untested, representing a major functional gap in the communication toolset.

requirement.text: The Clinical Decision Support (CDS) engine shall display a modal warning when a newly prescribed medication conflicts with the patient's documented active allergies or historical adverse reactions.

test_cases.description: Verify CDS warning triggers for active allergy conflicts.
test_cases.setup: EHR test environment; patient with documented active Penicillin allergy; test provider logged in.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Open patient chart.
Step: 2. Navigate to Order Entry.
Step: 3. Attempt to prescribe Amoxicillin (cross-reactivity with Penicillin).
Step: 4. Proceed to sign order.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. A modal warning interrupts the workflow.
ExpectedResult: 2. Modal clearly states the conflict with the active Penicillin allergy.
ExpectedResult: 3. Provider must enter an override reason to proceed.

rationale: The test suite validates the modal warning for active allergies but fails to test the functional requirement of triggering the CDS warning for historical adverse reactions, missing a critical patient safety pathway.

requirement.text: The scheduling module shall automatically run an electronic eligibility check (ANSI 270/271) with the primary payer and secondary payer (if applicable) 48 hours prior to the appointment.

test_cases.description: Verify automated eligibility check triggers for primary insurance.
test_cases.setup: Scheduling module test environment; clearinghouse stub active; patient scheduled for an appointment in exactly 48 hours; patient has primary and secondary insurance on file.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Advance system clock to the 48-hour pre-appointment threshold.
Step: 2. Monitor the automated batch job queue.
Step: 3. Check the patient's appointment record for eligibility status.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. An ANSI 270 request is generated and sent to the clearinghouse for the primary payer.
ExpectedResult: 2. An ANSI 271 response is received.
ExpectedResult: 3. Appointment status updates to 'Primary Ins: Verified'.

rationale: The functional coverage is incomplete as the test suite only verifies eligibility checks against the primary payer, completely ignoring the required secondary payer verification workflow.

requirement.text: Upon scanning a patient wristband, the Medication Administration Record (MAR) module must display the patient's full name, DOB, and all pending medications for the current 12-hour shift.

test_cases.description: Verify patient identification is displayed upon wristband scan.
test_cases.setup: Mobile MAR test device; barcode scanner active; test patient wristband barcode generated; test nurse logged in.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Log into the MAR app.
Step: 2. Use device camera to scan the patient's wristband barcode.
Step: 3. Observe the rendered patient header on the screen.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. System successfully decodes the barcode.
ExpectedResult: 2. The patient's full name is displayed accurately.
ExpectedResult: 3. The patient's DOB is displayed and matches the record.

rationale: The test verifies patient identification (Name, DOB) upon scanning but misses the core clinical function of displaying the pending medications for the current shift, which is the primary purpose of the MAR module.

requirement.text: The automated discharge summary module must compile the patient's admission diagnosis, discharge medications, and follow-up care instructions into a single printable PDF.

test_cases.description: Verify discharge PDF generation includes diagnosis and follow-up instructions.
test_cases.setup: Inpatient module test environment; patient status set to 'Pending Discharge'; required clinical documentation completed.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Navigate to the Discharge planning tab.
Step: 2. Click 'Generate Discharge Summary'.
Step: 3. Open the resulting PDF document.
Step: 4. Inspect document contents.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. A PDF document is successfully generated without errors.
ExpectedResult: 2. The 'Admission Diagnosis' section is populated correctly.
ExpectedResult: 3. The 'Follow-up Care Instructions' section is present and formatted.

rationale: The generated PDF is tested for diagnosis and follow-up instructions, but the test suite fails to verify the inclusion of discharge medications, representing a severe omission of functional requirement coverage.

requirement.text: For HIPAA compliance, the system must maintain an audit log of every user's access to a patient record, recording the user ID, timestamp, and the specific module viewed (e.g., Demographics, Clinical Notes).

test_cases.description: Verify user ID and timestamp are captured in the PHI access audit log.
test_cases.setup: System admin tools enabled; test user logged in; test patient chart available.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Test user opens the test patient's chart.
Step: 2. Log out as test user.
Step: 3. Log in as Security Admin.
Step: 4. Query the audit log database for the patient's MRN.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. An audit log entry exists for the access event.
ExpectedResult: 2. The log accurately reflects the test user's ID.
ExpectedResult: 3. The log captures the exact timestamp of access.

rationale: The audit log tests verify user ID and timestamp capture but completely omit verifying the functional logging of the specific module viewed (e.g., Demographics vs. Clinical Notes), leaving a compliance gap in PHI tracking.

requirement.text: The patient engagement system shall send automated appointment reminders via SMS, Email, and Voice Call 24 hours prior to the scheduled visit, respecting the patient's stated communication preference.

test_cases.description: Verify SMS and Email appointment reminders are dispatched.
test_cases.setup: Patient engagement test environment; two test patients configured—one with SMS preference, one with Email preference; appointments scheduled 24 hours out.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Trigger the automated 24-hour reminder job.
Step: 2. Check the communications outbound queue.
Step: 3. Verify delivery logs for both simulated endpoints.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. SMS reminder is queued and sent to Patient 1.
ExpectedResult: 2. Email reminder is queued and sent to Patient 2.
ExpectedResult: 3. Message templates contain correct appointment time and location.

rationale: The test suite successfully verifies SMS and Email reminder routing, but lacks functional coverage for the automated Voice Call reminder option, failing to validate the full spectrum of supported communication preferences.

requirement.text: The EHR system shall allow registrars to create a new patient profile using a unique Social Security Number (SSN) and Date of Birth (DOB).

test_cases.description: Verify successful creation of a patient profile using a valid, unique SSN and properly formatted DOB.
test_cases.setup: EHR test environment; test user logged in with Registrar role.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Navigate to 'Register New Patient' module.
Step: 2. Enter a valid, unique 9-digit SSN.
Step: 3. Enter a valid DOB in MM/DD/YYYY format.
Step: 4. Submit the registration form.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. System accepts the inputs.
ExpectedResult: 2. A new patient profile is generated with a unique Patient ID.
ExpectedResult: 3. Success confirmation is displayed to the registrar.

rationale: The test suite fully verifies the functional happy path for patient creation, but is missing critical negative coverage for error handling when a duplicate SSN is entered or an invalid DOB format is submitted.

requirement.text: The e-Prescribing module shall calculate age-appropriate medication dosages and warn the provider if a prescribed dose exceeds the FDA recommended maximum.

test_cases.description: Verify the system allows a normal, age-appropriate dosage to be prescribed without warnings.
test_cases.setup: Provider logged into e-Prescribing sandbox; active pediatric patient chart open.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Select Amoxicillin from the formulary.
Step: 2. Enter a standard dosage of 40mg/kg/day for a 5-year-old.
Step: 3. Click 'Send to Pharmacy'.
test_cases.expectedResults: ExpectedResult: 1. Prescription is queued successfully without any warning prompts.

test_cases.description: Verify the system triggers a warning when the maximum dosage is exceeded.
test_cases.setup: Provider logged into e-Prescribing sandbox; active pediatric patient chart open.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Select Amoxicillin from the formulary.
Step: 2. Enter a high dosage of 120mg/kg/day for a 5-year-old.
Step: 3. Click 'Send to Pharmacy'.
test_cases.expectedResults: ExpectedResult: 1. An FDA maximum dosage warning modal is displayed to the provider.

rationale: Functional and boundary behaviors (triggering the warning) are tested, but negative coverage is missing for scenarios where the provider attempts to bypass the warning without mandatory justification, or inputs a clearly invalid/alphanumeric dosage.

requirement.text: The application must synchronize real-time patient vitals from connected bedside monitors via standard HL7 v2.x messaging.

test_cases.description: Verify that a valid HL7 ORU^R01 message updates the patient vitals dashboard in real-time.
test_cases.setup: Application connected to HL7 message simulator; patient dashboard open on client browser.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Inject a valid HL7 ORU^R01 message containing Heart Rate (85 bpm) and SpO2 (98%) for the active patient.
Step: 2. Observe the bedside dashboard.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. The dashboard updates within 2 seconds.
ExpectedResult: 2. Heart Rate displays as 85 and SpO2 displays as 98%.

rationale: The suite adequately covers the functional synchronization of valid data, but lacks negative coverage for system stability when receiving malformed HL7 messages, unsupported message types, or network connection timeouts.

requirement.text: The system shall allow nursing staff to generate a shift handoff report and electronically sign it to transfer care responsibility.

test_cases.description: Verify nursing staff can generate and sign the shift handoff report successfully.
test_cases.setup: Test environment with two active nurse accounts (Nurse A and Nurse B); Nurse A assigned to Patient X.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Nurse A logs in and navigates to Patient X's chart.
Step: 2. Select 'Generate Handoff Report'.
Step: 3. Review populated data and click 'Sign & Transfer'.
Step: 4. Enter credentials to apply e-signature and assign Nurse B.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Report is generated with current shift data.
ExpectedResult: 2. E-signature is applied successfully.
ExpectedResult: 3. Care responsibility transfers to Nurse B in the system.

rationale: Functional flow is verified, but there is a clear gap in negative coverage regarding concurrent editing attempts by multiple nurses, or attempting to sign off when mandatory care tasks are left undocumented.

requirement.text: The EHR shall automatically trigger a critical alert to the attending physician's pager API when troponin lab results exceed the acute threshold of 0.04 ng/mL.

test_cases.description: Verify alert is triggered when troponin is above threshold.
test_cases.setup: EHR lab interface active; mock pager API configured to log requests.
test_cases.steps: Step: 1. Ingest a lab result for the patient with Troponin = 0.05 ng/mL.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. System detects threshold breach.
ExpectedResult: 2. Pager API receives the critical alert payload.

test_cases.description: Verify alert is NOT triggered when troponin is below threshold.
test_cases.setup: EHR lab interface active; mock pager API configured to log requests.
test_cases.steps: Step: 1. Ingest a lab result for the patient with Troponin = 0.02 ng/mL.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. System processes the result normally.
ExpectedResult: 2. No request is sent to the Pager API.

rationale: The test suite covers functional and boundary conditions for the threshold logic, but fails to provide negative coverage for scenarios where the lab result data feed is corrupted, missing units of measure, or the pager API returns a 500 server error.

requirement.text: To comply with HIPAA regulations, the clinical portal must automatically log out the user and mask the screen after 15 minutes of input inactivity.

test_cases.description: Verify the portal logs out and masks the screen after exactly 15 minutes of inactivity.
test_cases.setup: Clinical portal running in a fresh browser session; test user logged in.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. User logs into the portal.
Step: 2. Leave the mouse and keyboard untouched for 15 minutes and 1 second.
Step: 3. Observe the application state.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. The screen is immediately masked with a privacy overlay.
ExpectedResult: 2. The user session is terminated (requires full re-authentication to proceed).

rationale: Verifies the functional timeout mechanism, but completely lacks negative coverage testing against malicious attempts to bypass the masked screen (e.g., URL manipulation) or handling continuous automated polling scripts that simulate fake activity.

requirement.text: The Medication Administration Record (MAR) module shall verify the 5 Rights of Medication Administration by requiring a successful scan of both the patient wristband barcode and the medication barcode prior to administration.

test_cases.description: Verify medication administration is permitted when matching patient and medication barcodes are scanned.
test_cases.setup: Tablet device with barcode scanner active; patient wristband barcode generated; correct medication barcode generated.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Nurse opens the MAR module for the scheduled administration.
Step: 2. Scan the patient wristband barcode.
Step: 3. Scan the medication barcode.
Step: 4. Click 'Administer'.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Both scans register as 'Verified' in green.
ExpectedResult: 2. The 'Administer' button becomes enabled.
ExpectedResult: 3. Medication is successfully logged in the patient's record.

rationale: The happy-path administration flow is well-tested, but the suite is missing critical negative coverage for mismatched patient/medication barcodes or handling unreadable/damaged barcode inputs.

requirement.text: The telehealth module shall establish a secure, encrypted video consultation session between a provider and a patient using WebRTC.

test_cases.description: Verify a WebRTC video session can be established successfully between provider and patient endpoints.
test_cases.setup: Provider on desktop browser; Patient on mobile browser; appointment scheduled and active.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Provider clicks 'Start Session'.
Step: 2. Patient clicks 'Join Session'.
Step: 3. Both users accept browser camera/microphone permissions.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Secure WebRTC handshake completes.
ExpectedResult: 2. Provider and Patient can see and hear each other with latency under 200ms.

rationale: Functional connectivity is validated, but there is no negative coverage for adverse conditions such as dropped network connections mid-call, denied camera/microphone OS permissions, or unsupported legacy browser errors.

requirement.text: The hospital billing system shall verify patient insurance eligibility in real-time by querying the regional clearinghouse API using the subscriber ID.

test_cases.description: Verify the billing system correctly displays active insurance eligibility for a valid subscriber ID.
test_cases.setup: Billing module connected to clearinghouse sandbox API.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Navigate to 'Insurance Verification' tab.
Step: 2. Enter a known valid Subscriber ID (e.g., SUB-ACTIVE-999).
Step: 3. Click 'Verify Eligibility'.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. System queries the API successfully.
ExpectedResult: 2. Status 'ACTIVE' is returned and displayed alongside coverage details.

rationale: Verifies the functional happy path of checking eligibility, but lacks negative coverage for scenarios like receiving an 'expired policy' response payload, malformed XML/JSON from the clearinghouse, or handling an API gateway timeout.

requirement.text: The application shall capture and securely store biometric electronic signatures from patients to execute surgical consent forms.

test_cases.description: Verify a patient can apply a biometric signature to a surgical consent form and it is stored securely.
test_cases.setup: EHR connected to a biometric signature pad; unsigned surgical consent form queued.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Open the surgical consent form.
Step: 2. Hand the signature pad to the patient.
Step: 3. Patient signs the pad and presses 'Accept'.
Step: 4. Click 'Save Form' in the EHR.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Signature image and biometric metadata are captured.
ExpectedResult: 2. Form status changes to 'Consented'.
ExpectedResult: 3. Document is locked and securely archived in the patient repository.

rationale: The functional capture and storage of the signature is verified, but negative coverage is missing for scenarios where the hardware signature device disconnects mid-signing or the patient explicitly declines/cancels the consent flow.

requirement.text: The system shall calculate patient dosage for Medication X based on body weight (mg/kg), allowing weights from 2.0 kg to 150.0 kg.

test_cases.description: Verify calculation for nominal adult weight.
test_cases.setup: Medication X calculation module loaded; valid test patient profile.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Enter weight as 50.0 kg.
Step: 2. Request dosage calculation.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. System calculates dosage correctly based on 50.0 kg.
ExpectedResult: 2. System accepts input without errors.

test_cases.description: Verify calculation for higher nominal weight.
test_cases.setup: Medication X calculation module loaded; valid test patient profile.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Enter weight as 100.0 kg.
Step: 2. Request dosage calculation.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. System calculates dosage correctly based on 100.0 kg.
ExpectedResult: 2. System accepts input without errors.

rationale: The test suite verifies nominal functional paths but lacks boundary/edge coverage for the absolute minimum (2.0 kg) and maximum (150.0 kg) allowable weight limits, as well as out-of-bounds rejection (1.9 kg, 150.1 kg).

requirement.text: The EHR portal must automatically time out and log out the user after exactly 15 minutes of inactivity.

test_cases.description: Verify user remains logged in under the timeout limit.
test_cases.setup: EHR portal running in test environment; test user account.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Log into the EHR portal.
Step: 2. Remain inactive for 5 minutes.
Step: 3. Attempt to navigate to a new patient record.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. System allows navigation.
ExpectedResult: 2. User is not logged out.

test_cases.description: Verify user is logged out well past the timeout limit.
test_cases.setup: EHR portal running in test environment; test user account.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Log into the EHR portal.
Step: 2. Remain inactive for 20 minutes.
Step: 3. Attempt to navigate or interact with the page.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. System prompts that session has expired.
ExpectedResult: 2. User is redirected to the login screen.

rationale: Missing edge/boundary coverage validating the exact timeout threshold of 15 minutes (e.g., verifying behavior at exactly 14m 59s vs. 15m 01s).

requirement.text: Blood pressure readings shall be categorized as 'Hypertensive Crisis' if Systolic > 180 mmHg OR Diastolic > 120 mmHg.

test_cases.description: Verify normal blood pressure categorization.
test_cases.setup: Vitals entry module active; test patient record loaded.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Enter Systolic 120, Diastolic 80.
Step: 2. Save reading.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Reading is saved successfully.
ExpectedResult: 2. Category is marked as 'Normal'.

test_cases.description: Verify clear hypertensive crisis categorization.
test_cases.setup: Vitals entry module active; test patient record loaded.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Enter Systolic 200, Diastolic 130.
Step: 2. Save reading.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Reading is saved successfully.
ExpectedResult: 2. Category is marked as 'Hypertensive Crisis' with appropriate UI alerts.

rationale: The suite lacks boundary coverage for values exactly at the threshold limits (e.g., Systolic 180, Diastolic 120) to ensure the strict greater-than logic does not incorrectly trigger the crisis state.

requirement.text: The system must support importing patient records via CSV files up to a maximum size of 50MB.

test_cases.description: Verify successful import of small CSV file.
test_cases.setup: Import module ready; test dataset of 1MB CSV file available.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Navigate to patient import screen.
Step: 2. Upload 1MB CSV file.
Step: 3. Click Import.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. File uploads successfully.
ExpectedResult: 2. Patient records are imported without error.

test_cases.description: Verify rejection of completely invalid file type.
test_cases.setup: Import module ready; test dataset of 10MB PDF file available.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Navigate to patient import screen.
Step: 2. Attempt to upload 10MB PDF file.
Step: 3. Click Import.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. System rejects file upload.
ExpectedResult: 2. Error message indicates invalid file format.

rationale: Missing boundary test cases verifying the exact maximum file size limit (exactly 50MB) and the immediately out-of-bounds size (e.g., 50.1MB) to verify proper rejection.

requirement.text: A pediatric patient is defined as an individual under the age of 18 years old (0-17) at the time of admission.

test_cases.description: Verify 5-year-old is classified as pediatric.
test_cases.setup: Admissions module active; system date set to 2026-04-03.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Enter patient DOB as 2021-04-03.
Step: 2. Complete admission flow.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Patient is classified as pediatric.
ExpectedResult: 2. Pediatric protocols are assigned.

test_cases.description: Verify 25-year-old is classified as adult.
test_cases.setup: Admissions module active; system date set to 2026-04-03.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Enter patient DOB as 2001-04-03.
Step: 2. Complete admission flow.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Patient is classified as an adult.
ExpectedResult: 2. Standard adult protocols are assigned.

rationale: Missing critical boundary coverage testing the exact age transition, specifically a patient who is 17 years and 364 days old versus a patient exactly 18 years old on the day of admission.

requirement.text: The scheduling module allows booking appointments up to 365 days in advance, but not further.

test_cases.description: Verify booking for tomorrow.
test_cases.setup: Scheduling calendar open; valid provider availability.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Select a time slot 1 day in the future.
Step: 2. Confirm booking details and submit.
test_cases.expectedResults: ExpectedResult: 1. Appointment is successfully booked.

test_cases.description: Verify booking one month out.
test_cases.setup: Scheduling calendar open; valid provider availability.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Select a time slot 30 days in the future.
Step: 2. Confirm booking details and submit.
test_cases.expectedResults: ExpectedResult: 1. Appointment is successfully booked.

rationale: The test suite verifies standard functional scheduling but is entirely missing boundary coverage for the maximum limit (booking exactly 365 days out vs. rejection at 366 days).

requirement.text: Patient resting heart rate alarms shall trigger if the heart rate drops below 40 bpm or rises above 130 bpm for 3 consecutive readings.

test_cases.description: Verify alarm triggers for extreme low heart rate.
test_cases.setup: Telemetry simulator connected to patient monitor; baseline HR at 70 bpm.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Drop simulated HR to 30 bpm.
Step: 2. Hold at 30 bpm for 4 consecutive readings.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Alarm does not trigger on reading 1 or 2.
ExpectedResult: 2. Alarm triggers immediately upon the 3rd consecutive reading.

test_cases.description: Verify alarm triggers for extreme high heart rate.
test_cases.setup: Telemetry simulator connected to patient monitor; baseline HR at 70 bpm.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Raise simulated HR to 150 bpm.
Step: 2. Hold at 150 bpm for 4 consecutive readings.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Alarm does not trigger on reading 1 or 2.
ExpectedResult: 2. Alarm triggers immediately upon the 3rd consecutive reading.

rationale: Fails to cover boundary conditions for the exact numerical alarm thresholds (e.g., exactly 40 bpm and 130 bpm) to ensure inclusive/exclusive logic is handled correctly.

requirement.text: The pharmacy inventory system alerts staff when the stock of Schedule II controlled substances drops to exactly 10 units or fewer.

test_cases.description: Verify no alert for high stock levels.
test_cases.setup: Pharmacy inventory module loaded; Schedule II item (Oxycodone) initialized at 20 units.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Dispense 5 units.
Step: 2. Check system alerts.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Remaining stock shows 15 units.
ExpectedResult: 2. No low stock alert is generated.

test_cases.description: Verify alert triggers for very low stock.
test_cases.setup: Pharmacy inventory module loaded; Schedule II item (Oxycodone) initialized at 8 units.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Dispense 3 units.
Step: 2. Check system alerts.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Remaining stock shows 5 units.
ExpectedResult: 2. Low stock alert is actively displayed for the item.

rationale: Missing edge case coverage verifying the exact boundary condition where stock transitions from 11 (no alert expected) to exactly 10 (alert expected).

requirement.text: Patient portal passwords must contain between 8 and 64 characters, including at least one number and one special character.

test_cases.description: Verify password missing a number is rejected.
test_cases.setup: Patient portal account creation screen.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Enter password: 'Password!'.
Step: 2. Submit form.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Form is rejected.
ExpectedResult: 2. Error message states a number is required.

test_cases.description: Verify valid mid-length password is accepted.
test_cases.setup: Patient portal account creation screen.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Enter password: 'ValidPass123!'.
Step: 2. Submit form.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Password is accepted.
ExpectedResult: 2. Account creation proceeds.

rationale: While functional and negative coverage for character types exists, the suite is entirely missing boundary coverage for the length constraints (exactly 8 chars, exactly 64 chars, and invalid lengths of 7 or 65 chars).

requirement.text: The patient copay calculator deducts a flat $20.00 for in-network visits unless the patient's remaining out-of-pocket (OOP) maximum is less than $20.00, in which case it deducts the exact remaining amount.

test_cases.description: Verify standard flat copay deduction.
test_cases.setup: Billing module; patient has $1000 remaining on OOP max.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Initiate in-network visit billing.
Step: 2. Calculate copay.
test_cases.expectedResults: ExpectedResult: 1. Calculated copay is exactly $20.00.

test_cases.description: Verify fractional copay deduction when near OOP max.
test_cases.setup: Billing module; patient has $5.00 remaining on OOP max.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Initiate in-network visit billing.
Step: 2. Calculate copay.
test_cases.expectedResults: ExpectedResult: 1. Calculated copay is exactly $5.00.

rationale: Missing boundary coverage for the pivot point where the remaining OOP maximum is exactly the standard copay amount ($20.00), or slightly above/below ($20.01, $19.99).

requirement.text: The patient portal shall allow a registered patient to schedule an appointment with a primary care physician, enforcing a strict minimum 24-hour advance notice from the current system time.

test_cases.description: Verify functional path: scheduling an appointment exactly 48 hours in advance.
test_cases.setup: Test patient account created and logged in; PCP schedule has availability exactly 48 hours from current system time.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Navigate to 'Schedule Appointment'.
Step: 2. Select assigned PCP.
Step: 3. Select the time slot 48 hours in the future.
Step: 4. Confirm appointment.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Appointment is successfully created.
ExpectedResult: 2. Confirmation screen displays correct date and time.

test_cases.description: Verify boundary path: scheduling an appointment exactly 24 hours in advance.
test_cases.setup: Test patient logged in; PCP schedule manipulated to show availability exactly 24.0 hours from current system time.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Navigate to 'Schedule Appointment'.
Step: 2. Select PCP.
Step: 3. Select the time slot exactly 24 hours in the future.
Step: 4. Confirm appointment.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. System allows the selection.
ExpectedResult: 2. Appointment is successfully created.

test_cases.description: Verify boundary path: attempting to schedule an appointment 23 hours and 59 minutes in advance.
test_cases.setup: Test patient logged in; PCP schedule manipulated to show availability 23 hours and 59 minutes from current system time.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Navigate to 'Schedule Appointment'.
Step: 2. Select PCP.
Step: 3. Attempt to select the time slot 23h 59m in the future.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. The time slot is grayed out or unselectable.
ExpectedResult: 2. If forced via API, backend rejects with '24-hour notice required' HTTP 400 error.

test_cases.description: Verify negative path: database timeout during appointment confirmation.
test_cases.setup: Test patient logged in; valid time slot selected; database write access temporarily suspended (timeout simulation).
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Proceed to confirm a valid appointment.
Step: 2. Click 'Confirm'.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. System displays a graceful 'Service temporarily unavailable' message.
ExpectedResult: 2. No partial or phantom appointment records are generated.

rationale: This test suite fully verifies the requirement by covering the primary functional path, validating the exact threshold boundaries (24h vs 23h59m), and including a negative scenario to ensure robust error handling under system duress, leaving zero coverage gaps.

requirement.text: The e-prescribing module shall automatically block the issuance of a prescription if a 'Severe' drug-drug interaction is detected against the patient's active medication list.

test_cases.description: Verify functional path: blocking a prescription when a known severe interaction exists.
test_cases.setup: Patient active med list contains Warfarin; provider logged in; interaction database online.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Navigate to e-prescribing module for the patient.
Step: 2. Search and select 'Amiodarone' (known severe interaction with Warfarin).
Step: 3. Attempt to sign and send prescription.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. System triggers a hard-stop alert indicating a 'Severe' interaction.
ExpectedResult: 2. The 'Sign and Send' button is disabled.
ExpectedResult: 3. Prescription is not transmitted to the pharmacy.

test_cases.description: Verify boundary path: allowing a prescription with a 'Moderate' interaction, accompanied by a warning.
test_cases.setup: Patient active med list contains Lisinopril; provider logged in.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Select 'Ibuprofen' (known moderate interaction with Lisinopril).
Step: 2. Attempt to sign and send.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. System displays a 'Moderate' interaction warning.
ExpectedResult: 2. Provider is allowed to acknowledge the warning and proceed.
ExpectedResult: 3. Prescription is successfully transmitted.

test_cases.description: Verify boundary path: patient with an empty active medication list.
test_cases.setup: Patient has no active medications on file; provider logged in.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Prescribe a high-risk medication (e.g., Warfarin).
Step: 2. Sign and send.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Interaction check runs but finds zero conflicts.
ExpectedResult: 2. Prescription is successfully transmitted without alerts.

test_cases.description: Verify negative path: interaction database API is unreachable.
test_cases.setup: Provider logged in; drug-drug interaction external API service is taken offline.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Attempt to prescribe any medication.
Step: 2. Sign and send.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. System fails closed (safe mode).
ExpectedResult: 2. System blocks issuance and displays: 'Interaction check unavailable. Cannot proceed.'

rationale: This test suite fully verifies the requirement by addressing the functional blocking of severe interactions, confirming boundary logic (moderate vs severe; empty med lists), and ensuring fail-safe negative behavior when dependency services fail, providing complete coverage.

requirement.text: A patient's heart rate telemetry alert shall trigger a high-priority notification to the central nursing station only if the heart rate exceeds 120 bpm for more than 3 consecutive minutes.

test_cases.description: Verify functional path: HR exceeds 120 bpm for 4 minutes.
test_cases.setup: Telemetry simulator connected to patient profile; nursing station dashboard open.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Simulate HR at 125 bpm.
Step: 2. Maintain 125 bpm for 4 minutes.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. No alert for the first 3 minutes.
ExpectedResult: 2. At exactly 3 minutes and 1 second, a high-priority notification appears on the nursing dashboard.

test_cases.description: Verify boundary path: HR drops below threshold just before the time limit.
test_cases.setup: Telemetry simulator connected; nursing station dashboard open.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Simulate HR at 125 bpm for 2 minutes and 55 seconds.
Step: 2. Drop HR to 115 bpm at 2 minutes 56 seconds.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. The 3-minute timer resets.
ExpectedResult: 2. No high-priority notification is triggered.

test_cases.description: Verify boundary path: HR is exactly 120 bpm for 5 minutes.
test_cases.setup: Telemetry simulator connected; nursing station dashboard open.
test_cases.steps: Step: 1. Simulate HR exactly at 120 bpm for 5 minutes.
test_cases.expectedResults: ExpectedResult: 1. No alert is triggered (requirement states 'exceeds' 120, implying > 120).

test_cases.description: Verify negative path: telemetry sensor disconnected during high HR event.
test_cases.setup: Telemetry simulator connected; nursing station dashboard open.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Simulate HR at 130 bpm for 1 minute.
Step: 2. Simulate complete signal loss/sensor disconnect.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Medical high-priority HR alert is NOT triggered.
ExpectedResult: 2. A separate 'Technical/Equipment Warning' is triggered for the lost signal.

rationale: This suite fully verifies the requirement by rigorously testing the functional sustained threshold, targeting the exact mathematical boundaries (>120 vs =120, and the exact 3-minute cutoff), and handling negative edge cases (signal loss), ensuring 100% confidence in the alerting logic.

requirement.text: The patient portal password creation endpoint must enforce a minimum length of 12 characters, including at least one uppercase letter, one number, and one special character.

test_cases.description: Verify functional path: valid password creation meeting all criteria.
test_cases.setup: Unauthenticated user on password creation/reset screen.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Enter password 'ValidPass123!'.
Step: 2. Submit form.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Password is accepted.
ExpectedResult: 2. User account is successfully updated/created.

test_cases.description: Verify boundary path: password length exactly 12 characters.
test_cases.setup: Unauthenticated user on password creation screen.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Enter password 'Abcd!2345678'.
Step: 2. Submit form.
test_cases.expectedResults: ExpectedResult: 1. Password is accepted.

test_cases.description: Verify boundary path: password length exactly 11 characters.
test_cases.setup: Unauthenticated user on password creation screen.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Enter password 'Abcd!234567'.
Step: 2. Submit form.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Password is rejected.
ExpectedResult: 2. Error message specifies minimum 12 characters required.

test_cases.description: Verify boundary paths: missing specific required character types.
test_cases.setup: Unauthenticated user on password creation screen.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Attempt 'nocapitalletter1!' (missing uppercase).
Step: 2. Attempt 'NOCAPITALNUMBER!' (missing number).
Step: 3. Attempt 'NoSpecialChar123' (missing special).
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. All attempts are rejected.
ExpectedResult: 2. Specific error messages indicate the missing character type.

test_cases.description: Verify negative path: SQL injection / XSS payload in password field.
test_cases.setup: Unauthenticated user on password creation screen.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Enter password 'Password123!'; DROP TABLE users;--'.
Step: 2. Submit form.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Password may be accepted if length/char rules met, but input is sanitized.
ExpectedResult: 2. Database remains unaffected; payload is treated strictly as a string literal.

rationale: This test suite fully verifies the requirement by confirming the happy path, exhaustively testing all boundary conditions (length limits and individual character constraints), and including negative security testing for input sanitization, leaving no functional gaps.

requirement.text: The billing system shall automatically apply a 10% senior discount to the final invoice for uninsured patients aged 65 or older strictly calculated on the Date of Service.

test_cases.description: Verify functional path: uninsured 70-year-old patient.
test_cases.setup: Patient profile: Uninsured, DOB makes them 70 on Date of Service (DOS). Invoice generated for $100.
test_cases.steps: Step: 1. Process the invoice through the billing system.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. A 10% discount ($10) is automatically applied.
ExpectedResult: 2. Final invoice total is $90.

test_cases.description: Verify boundary path: uninsured patient turning 65 exactly on the Date of Service.
test_cases.setup: Patient profile: Uninsured, DOB is exactly 65 years prior to the DOS.
test_cases.steps: Step: 1. Process the invoice.
test_cases.expectedResults: ExpectedResult: 1. 10% discount is applied.

test_cases.description: Verify boundary path: uninsured patient turning 65 the day after the Date of Service.
test_cases.setup: Patient profile: Uninsured, DOB makes them 64 years and 364 days old on DOS.
test_cases.steps: Step: 1. Process the invoice.
test_cases.expectedResults: ExpectedResult: 1. No discount is applied; system strictly honors DOS age.

test_cases.description: Verify functional/negative path: insured patient aged 70.
test_cases.setup: Patient profile: Insured (Medicare active), age 70 on DOS.
test_cases.steps: Step: 1. Process the invoice.
test_cases.expectedResults: ExpectedResult: 1. No discount is applied, as the patient is insured.

test_cases.description: Verify negative path: patient profile missing DOB field entirely.
test_cases.setup: Patient profile: Uninsured, DOB field is NULL.
test_cases.steps: Step: 1. Process the invoice.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Billing process completes without crashing.
ExpectedResult: 2. No discount is applied.
ExpectedResult: 3. A warning is logged for missing demographic data.

rationale: This suite fully verifies the requirement by testing standard positive functionality, precise chronological boundary conditions (age exactly 65 vs 64/364 days on DOS), negative conflicting conditions (insured), and handling of null data, achieving comprehensive coverage.

requirement.text: The EHR system must automatically invalidate the session token and log out users after 15 minutes of inactivity to comply with HIPAA regulations.

test_cases.description: Verify functional path: automatic logout after 16 minutes of inactivity.
test_cases.setup: User logged into EHR; network traffic monitor active.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Perform an action to register activity.
Step: 2. Leave system completely idle for 16 minutes.
Step: 3. Attempt to navigate to a new patient record.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Session token is invalidated.
ExpectedResult: 2. User is redirected to the login screen.
ExpectedResult: 3. 'Session expired due to inactivity' message is displayed.

test_cases.description: Verify boundary path: activity at exactly 14 minutes and 59 seconds.
test_cases.setup: User logged into EHR.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Perform an action.
Step: 2. Wait exactly 14 minutes and 59 seconds.
Step: 3. Click a link in the EHR.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Action succeeds.
ExpectedResult: 2. Session timer is reset to 0.

test_cases.description: Verify boundary path: activity at exactly 15 minutes and 1 second.
test_cases.setup: User logged into EHR.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Perform an action.
Step: 2. Wait exactly 15 minutes and 1 second.
Step: 3. Click a link.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Action is intercepted.
ExpectedResult: 2. User is logged out and redirected to login.

test_cases.description: Verify negative path: attempting to reuse the expired token via direct API call.
test_cases.setup: User logged into EHR; API testing tool ready.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Extract active session token.
Step: 2. Wait 16 minutes.
Step: 3. Use API tool to send a GET request for PHI using the extracted token.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. API returns HTTP 401 Unauthorized.
ExpectedResult: 2. No PHI is returned, confirming backend token invalidation.

rationale: This test suite fully verifies the requirement by validating the end-to-end frontend logout, probing the exact temporal boundaries (14m59s vs 15m01s), and securing the negative path by ensuring backend token destruction prevents API bypass, yielding full coverage.

requirement.text: Lab results tagged as 'Critical' by the LIS (Laboratory Information System) must prompt for and successfully validate a Dual-Factor Authentication (MFA) token before a physician can view the result details.

test_cases.description: Verify functional path: successful MFA validation for critical labs.
test_cases.setup: Physician logged in; LIS has pushed a 'Critical' potassium lab result.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Click on the critical lab result notification.
Step: 2. MFA prompt appears. Enter valid 6-digit TOTP code.
Step: 3. Submit.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. MFA validates successfully.
ExpectedResult: 2. Lab result details are displayed.

test_cases.description: Verify boundary path: non-critical labs do not require MFA.
test_cases.setup: Physician logged in; LIS has pushed a 'Normal' CBC lab result.
test_cases.steps: Step: 1. Click on the normal lab result.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Lab details are displayed immediately.
ExpectedResult: 2. No MFA prompt is triggered.

test_cases.description: Verify negative path: invalid MFA code entered multiple times.
test_cases.setup: Physician logged in; attempting to view a critical lab.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Click on critical lab.
Step: 2. Enter incorrect MFA code 3 times in a row.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Access to lab is denied.
ExpectedResult: 2. After 3rd failed attempt, MFA mechanism is temporarily locked.
ExpectedResult: 3. Security alert logged.

test_cases.description: Verify negative path: direct URL browsing bypass attempt.
test_cases.setup: Physician logged in; knows the direct URL format for lab result details.
test_cases.steps: Step: 1. Paste direct URL to a critical lab result into the browser address bar without passing through MFA prompt.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Server-side validation detects missing MFA context.
ExpectedResult: 2. Request is redirected to the MFA challenge screen; no PHI is leaked.

rationale: This suite fully verifies the requirement by testing the core functional MFA gate, verifying the boundary criteria of lab criticality flags, and thoroughly testing negative bypass vectors (brute force and direct object reference), ensuring total security and functional coverage.

requirement.text: The telemedicine video module shall gracefully degrade the connection to an audio-only mode if the user's available bandwidth drops below 500 kbps, notifying the user of the change.

test_cases.description: Verify functional path: bandwidth drops to 300 kbps.
test_cases.setup: Active telemedicine session with both video and audio; network throttle tool active.
test_cases.steps: Step: 1. Throttle available bandwidth to 300 kbps.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Video feed stops transmitting/rendering.
ExpectedResult: 2. Audio feed continues without dropping the call.
ExpectedResult: 3. UI displays toast message: 'Low bandwidth. Switching to audio-only.'

test_cases.description: Verify boundary path: bandwidth drops to exactly 499 kbps.
test_cases.setup: Active telemedicine session; network throttle tool active.
test_cases.steps: Step: 1. Throttle bandwidth to exactly 499 kbps.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Module degrades to audio-only.
ExpectedResult: 2. Notification is displayed.

test_cases.description: Verify boundary path: bandwidth fluctuates but stays at 500 kbps or above.
test_cases.setup: Active telemedicine session; network throttle tool active.
test_cases.steps: Step: 1. Throttle bandwidth to exactly 500 kbps.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Both video and audio streams are maintained.
ExpectedResult: 2. No degradation occurs.

test_cases.description: Verify negative path: complete network loss (0 kbps).
test_cases.setup: Active telemedicine session.
test_cases.steps: Step: 1. Simulate hard network disconnect (airplane mode).
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Call does not hang in zombie state.
ExpectedResult: 2. UI displays 'Connection lost. Attempting to reconnect...' and terminates call gracefully after timeout.

rationale: This test suite fully verifies the requirement by testing standard functional degradation, mathematically precise bandwidth boundaries (499 vs 500 kbps), and negative absolute-failure states, providing an exhaustive evaluation of the module's resilience logic.

requirement.text: The blood bank inventory system must restrict the allocation of O-negative blood units strictly to patients tagged as 'Emergency Trauma', unless a digital override is executed by a user with the 'Blood Bank Director' role.

test_cases.description: Verify functional path: standard allocation for Emergency Trauma patient.
test_cases.setup: System user logged in; patient tagged as 'Emergency Trauma'.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Request 2 units of O-negative blood for the patient.
Step: 2. Submit allocation request.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. System approves allocation.
ExpectedResult: 2. Inventory is decremented by 2 units.

test_cases.description: Verify boundary/negative path: standard allocation attempt for a routine surgery patient.
test_cases.setup: System user logged in (standard nurse role); patient tagged as 'Routine Surgery'.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Request 1 unit of O-negative blood.
Step: 2. Submit allocation request.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. System blocks the allocation.
ExpectedResult: 2. Error shown: 'O-negative restricted to Emergency Trauma. Override required.'

test_cases.description: Verify functional path: Blood Bank Director overrides restriction for a routine patient.
test_cases.setup: User with 'Blood Bank Director' role logged in; patient tagged as 'Routine Surgery'.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Request 1 unit of O-negative blood.
Step: 2. System prompts restriction warning.
Step: 3. Click 'Director Override' and submit.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Override is accepted.
ExpectedResult: 2. Allocation is approved and logged in the audit trail.

test_cases.description: Verify negative path: unauthorized user attempts digital override API call.
test_cases.setup: Standard nurse logged in; intercept proxy active.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Submit allocation request for routine patient.
Step: 2. Intercept API call and inject {"director_override": true}.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Backend RBAC validation fails.
ExpectedResult: 2. Allocation is denied with HTTP 403 Forbidden.

rationale: This test suite fully verifies the requirement by confirming normal operational logic, testing the boundary of patient tagging restrictions, and ensuring robust negative security testing around Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) overrides, missing no functional dimensions.

requirement.text: The patient intake form must accept file uploads for insurance cards, restricted strictly to PDF and JPG MIME types, with a maximum file size of exactly 5.0 MB (5242880 bytes) per file.

test_cases.description: Verify functional path: uploading a valid 2MB PDF file.
test_cases.setup: Patient on intake form screen.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Select a valid PDF file sized 2.0 MB.
Step: 2. Click Upload.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. File uploads successfully.
ExpectedResult: 2. Preview or success indicator is shown.

test_cases.description: Verify boundary path: uploading a JPG file of exactly 5242880 bytes.
test_cases.setup: Patient on intake form screen; JPG file padded to exactly 5242880 bytes.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Select the 5.0 MB JPG file.
Step: 2. Click Upload.
test_cases.expectedResults: ExpectedResult: 1. File uploads successfully.

test_cases.description: Verify boundary path: uploading a file of 5242881 bytes.
test_cases.setup: Patient on intake form screen; PDF file sized exactly 5242881 bytes.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Select the file.
Step: 2. Click Upload.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Upload is blocked.
ExpectedResult: 2. Error message explicitly states 'File exceeds 5.0 MB limit'.

test_cases.description: Verify negative path: uploading an executable file renamed to .jpg.
test_cases.setup: Patient on intake form screen; malware.exe renamed to insurance.jpg.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Select 'insurance.jpg'.
Step: 2. Click Upload.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Upload is blocked by backend MIME type / magic number validation.
ExpectedResult: 2. Error message states 'Invalid file format'.

test_cases.description: Verify negative path: network interruption during upload.
test_cases.setup: Patient on intake form screen.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Select a 4MB PDF.
Step: 2. Click Upload.
Step: 3. Terminate network connection at 50% completion.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. UI gracefully handles the timeout.
ExpectedResult: 2. Backend does not save a corrupted/partial file to storage.

rationale: This suite fully verifies the requirement by handling standard inputs, strictly testing the exact byte boundaries for file size limitations, and evaluating negative security/stability risks (MIME spoofing and interrupted transfers), representing 100% test coverage.
